input:
# Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare

## Abbreviations

- AI: Artificial Intelligence
- LLM: Large Language Model
- NLP: Natural Language Processing

## Introduction

Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming healthcare. Large Language Models (LLM) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) are key enabling technologies.

AI enables faster diagnosis and triage. LLMs can process vast amounts of medical literature and summarise findings. NLP extracts structured insights from unstructured clinical notes.

## Conclusion

AI, LLM, and NLP will continue to shape the future of healthcare delivery.
target_answer: AI, LLM, and NLP each have an inline definition at their first occurrence on line 11 and all three are listed in the Abbreviations section. Each appears again on lines 13 and 17 without inline definitions. 9 total occurrences, all non-ignored.

input:
# Climate Change and Economic Growth

## Abbreviations

- CO2: Carbon Dioxide
- IPCC: Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change
- GDP: Gross Domestic Product

## Introduction

CO2 emissions continue to rise globally despite international agreements. The IPCC has issued increasingly urgent warnings about rising temperatures. GDP growth must be decoupled from greenhouse gas emissions.

## Methods

CO2 concentration levels were measured using satellite data. IPCC assessment reports from 2014 to 2023 were reviewed. GDP data was sourced from the World Bank open data portal.

## Conclusion

Decoupling CO2 emissions from GDP growth remains the central challenge of climate policy, and IPCC guidance will continue to inform national targets.
target_answer: CO2, IPCC, and GDP are all listed in the Abbreviations section but none have an inline definition at any occurrence — every occurrence has an empty inline_definition. Each appears 3 times (lines 11, 15, 19). 9 total occurrences, all non-ignored.

input:
# Cybersecurity Landscape Report

## Abbreviations

- TLS: Transport Layer Security

## Introduction

Transport Layer Security (TLS) is the foundational protocol for encrypted web communications. Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks have also grown significantly in scale and frequency.

## Threat Analysis

TLS version 1.3 introduced major improvements over prior versions. DDoS attacks increasingly target critical infrastructure and cloud services.

## Conclusion

Organisations must upgrade to TLS 1.3 and deploy DDoS mitigation strategies to maintain a secure posture.
target_answer: TLS has an inline definition at first use ('Transport Layer Security') and is listed in the Abbreviations section. DDoS has an inline definition at first use ('Distributed Denial of Service') but has no abbreviations_section_definition (not listed in the section). Both appear 3 times (lines 9, 13, 17). 6 total occurrences, all non-ignored.

input:
# Defense Technology Assessment

## Office of the Under Secretary of War (OUSW)

The OUSW was established in 1941 to coordinate wartime procurement. The OUSW played a pivotal role in managing contracts across all branches of the armed forces.

## Advanced Research Projects Agency (ARPA)

ARPA was founded in 1958 in response to the Soviet Sputnik launch. ARPA later became DARPA when the 'Defense' prefix was added.

## Conclusion

Both the OUSW and ARPA represent landmark moments in the institutional history of U.S. defense technology.

target_abbreviations.inline_definition: Office of the Under Secretary of War

target_answer: OUSW and ARPA first appear in headings (lines 3 and 7) with inline definitions — these heading occurrences should be marked ignored=true. Both appear twice on their next body lines (line 5 for OUSW, line 9 for ARPA) without inline definitions. DARPA appears once on line 9 without an inline definition. Line 13 contains OUSW (occurrence 4), ARPA (occurrence 4), and U.S. (occurrence 1) — all three must be recorded. U.S. is exempt (United States abbreviation) and should be marked ignored=true. No abbreviations section exists. 10 total occurrences.

input:
# Machine Learning in Drug Discovery

## Abbreviations

- ML: Machine Learning
- FDA: Food and Drug Administration
- API: Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient

## Introduction

Machine Learning (ML) is accelerating the drug discovery pipeline. Regulatory submissions to the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) must now account for ML-derived evidence. The Active Pharmaceutical Ingredient (API) characterisation phase is one area where ML models have proven particularly effective.

## Applications

ML models can screen millions of molecular candidates in hours. FDA guidance documents now address the validation of ML-based diagnostic tools. API purity testing has been automated using ML-driven spectroscopic analysis.

## References

- Smith, J. and Brown, A. (2022). ML Methods for API Synthesis Optimisation. Journal of Pharmaceutical Sciences, 111(4), 1200-1215.
- Jones, R. (2023). FDA Regulatory Frameworks for ML in Clinical Trials. Drug Safety, 46(2), 88-102.
target_answer: ML, FDA, and API all have inline definitions at first use and are listed in the Abbreviations section. ML appears 8 times total, FDA 3 times, API 3 times. Occurrences in the References section (lines 19-20) should be marked ignored=true. 14 total occurrences (10 non-ignored, 4 ignored).

input:
# Advances in Language Technology

## Abbreviations

- LLM: Large Language Model
- GPU: Graphics Processing Unit

## Introduction

Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed natural language processing. Training these models requires powerful Graphics Processing Units (GPUs).

## Methods

Each LLM was fine-tuned on domain-specific datasets. We used 8 GPUs per training run. The LLMs achieved state-of-the-art performance across all benchmarks.

## Conclusion

LLMs will continue to improve as GPU availability increases.
target_answer: Plural forms (LLMs, GPUs) should be treated as the same abbreviation as their singular forms (LLM, GPU) and recorded with the singular base form as the abbr value. 'Large Language Models (LLMs)' on line 10 is recorded as the inline definition for LLM. 'Graphics Processing Units (GPUs)' on line 10 is recorded as the inline definition for GPU. Both are listed in the Abbreviations section. LLM appears 4 times (lines 10, 14, 14, 18), GPU appears 3 times (lines 10, 14, 18). 7 total occurrences, all non-ignored.
